dialogos.texto_en: *yawn*
(In the end, I fell asleep and woke up in the morning.\n I slept well and my body feels refreshed, but...\n My mind still feels foggy.)
(Today's sightseeing spot is American Village, isn't it.\n ...I need to pull myself together.)
(Last night, I didn't have complete confidence,\n but I realized my feelings a little bit...)
(I only have three days left.\n I need to figure out my true feelings\n for them, and give my answer.)
Alright, time to psych myself up.
Good morning.
Yo.\nGood mo%p-2;――
Huh?
Good morning.\nIt's a beautiful day for sightseeing today, isn't it?
Did you sleep well last night?
Well, I guess...
Why are you making that face this morning?
If you're still feeling groggy, go wash your face.
In the meantime,\nlet's decide what we want to do for breakfast.
H-hey, Itsuki?\nStop joking around, okay?
What do you mean by joking around?
You must be hungry too, Uesugi-kun!
Indeed.\nThe start of the day\nshould always begin with a delicious breakfast.
We had Okinawan cuisine last night,\nso what should we have today?
Hey...\nIf you're not joking, then what's going on?
What's with this nightmare?\nCould I still be dreaming?
It's no dream, you know.
...I see, this is quite troublesome.\nIt makes me wonder what's going to happen next.
Do you still not understand, Uesugi-kun?
Could it be...\nThis is the trial you were talking about yesterday?
Even so, to come this far\nonly to end up playing your famous quintuplets game.
Surely you don't think so%p-2;――\n%p;but, by any chance, can you not tell who it is\nyou have feelings for?
Of course, THAT'S not possible.
What're your intentions?\nSurely, there must be a purpose behind all of this.
Yes, that's correct.
You already know what it is, don't you?
...I see.\nSo that's what you meant by a trial.
Even so, to come this far\nonly to end up playing your famous quintuplets game.
Surely you don't think so%p-2;――\n%p;but, by any chance, can you not tell who it is\nyou have feelings for?
Of course, THAT'S not possible.
I see, so this is the trial, huh?
(If I'm going to choose someone,\n then I need to try and figure out who it is...)
I understand now.\nSo what you were saying about\n'testing my resolve' was about this.
Indeed.
You're as sharp as ever,\nUesugi-kun.\nI appreciate your straightforwardness.
Even so, to come this far\nonly to end up playing your famous quintuplets game.
Surely you don't think so%p-2;――\n%p;but, by any chance, can you not tell who it is\nyou have feelings for?
Of course, THAT'S not possible.
Yes, that's right.\nIf there is love,\nthen you should be able to tell.
Your reasoning is as nonsensical as ever, but...\nI feel like I understand your feelings now.
So, can you tell who is who then?
Naturally, this is a test that's unique to only you quintuplets.\nAlright, let's do this thing.
Understood.\nThen, let's start the quintuplets game, Okinawa version.
I'll explain the rules.
Alright, please.
The final day will be your last chance to solve this\nquintuplets game... In other words, the deadline\nwill be the evening of the day after tomorrow.
So that means there's three days, including today.
Yes.\nWe'll be taking the plane back home in the morning\nof the sixth day, so please keep that in mind.
Yeah, we're flying back that day, so that's obvious.
The rest is simple. On the evening of the final day,\nplease confess to the person you have chosen\nbased on your answer.
...Got it.
However.
If you confess to the wrong person,\nthen the game will be considered a failure.
What happens if I fail?
Needless to say, your confession will be invalidated.\nUnderstood?
(Originally, I intended to use these three days\n to figure out my own feelings. My task hasn't changed.)
(If there is love, then I should be able to tell%p-2;――\n%p; Hmph, a perfect challenge!)
Understood.\nI'll accept your game, fair and square.
Then, let's begin the quintuplets game.\nLet's enjoy Okinawa sightseeing today as well,\nUesugi-kun.
